difficulty_levels.name: multiple_choice
difficulty_levels.description: Choose the correct translation or best matching sentence.

difficulty_levels.name: word_bank
difficulty_levels.description: Build the sentence from pre-given words.

tips.content: Use the accusative after the verb 'mieć' (to have). Also, 'wesele' means a wedding reception, while 'ślub' means wedding.
Use the accusative after the verb 'lubić' (to like).
'Meble' (furniture) is a plural noun in Polish. Its singular form is 'mebel' (a piece of furniture).
When combining two colours into one mixed colour, the first adjective should end in -o and the two colours are joined with a hyphen.
The flag of Poland is 'flaga Polski'. Polish uses the declined form 'Polski' here, not 'Polska'.
If you want to say that you appreciate the look of something or somebody, it is often better to use 'podoba mi się' rather than 'lubię'.
Use the accusative after the verb 'kupić' (to buy).
The verb 'pasować' can mean to match, suit, or fit. With a singular noun, use the 3rd-person singular form 'pasuje'; with a plural noun, use 'pasują'.
examples: Ten sweter na mnie nie pasuje, jest za duży. = This sweater doesn't fit me, it's too big.
Ten niebieski krawat idealnie pasuje do twojej koszuli. = This blue tie matches your shirt perfectly.
Ten kolor ci pasuje. = This colour suits you.

tips.content: 'Włos' is the singular form of 'hair' (one hair). Hair on your head is usually expressed with the plural noun 'włosy'.
examples: Jej włosy są piękne. = Her hair is beautiful.

tips.content: 'Nie stać mnie na...' means 'I can't afford...'. It is followed by the accusative.
examples: Nie stać mnie na nowy samochód. = I can't afford a new car.

sentences.en: I have a blue dress for the wedding reception.
Brown and purple cars aren't very popular.
sentences.pl: Brązowe i fioletowe samochody nie są bardzo popularne.
english_variants: Brown and purple cars are not very popular.

sentences.en: The beige furniture in the living room looks beautiful.
sentences.pl: Beżowe meble w salonie wyglądają pięknie.

sentences.en: The flag of Poland is red and white (lit. white-red).
sentences.literal_en: The flag of Poland is white-red.
english_variants: The flag of Poland is red and white.

sentences.en: I like this pink lipstick. I like light colours.
sentences.pl: Podoba mi się ta różowa szminka. Lubię jasne kolory.
polish_alternatives: Podoba mi się ta różowa pomadka. Lubię jasne kolory.

sentences.en: I'm planning to buy a navy-blue jacket for autumn.
sentences.pl: Planuję kupić granatową kurtkę na jesień.

sentences.en: In my opinion, yellow will work (lit. be) better than purple.
sentences.pl: Moim zdaniem żółty będzie lepszy niż fioletowy.
sentences.literal_en: In my opinion, yellow will be better than purple.
english_variants: In my opinion, yellow will work better than purple.

sentences.en: "What's your favourite colour?" "I think it is (lit. I think that) pink or yellow."
sentences.pl: – Jaki jest twój ulubiony kolor? – Myślę, że różowy albo/lub żółty.
sentences.literal_en: I think that pink or yellow.
english_variants: What's your favourite colour? I think pink or yellow.

sentences.en: I think that orange and navy-blue match quite well.
sentences.pl: Myślę, że pomarańczowy i granatowy pasują całkiem dobrze.

sentences.en: Oh no! My first grey hair. I can't believe it!
sentences.pl: O nie! Moje pierwsze siwe włosy. Nie mogę w to uwierzyć!
english_variants: Oh no! My first grey hairs. I can't believe it!

sentences.en: Is this white pen drive yours?
I can't afford a new suit. Do you think my old green one will be good?
sentences.pl: Nie stać mnie na nowy garnitur. (Czy) myślisz, że mój stary zielony będzie dobry?
polish_alternatives: Nie stać mnie na nowy garnitur. Myślisz, że mój stary zielony będzie dobry?

sentences.en: I like wearing this brown sweater/jumper.

generated_practice.prompt_en: Choose the best Polish translation of: 'Do you like dark chocolate?'
Build: 'The flag of Poland is red and white.'
Translate into Polish: 'I like wearing this brown sweater.'
